data.title: Add TLBI (TLB Invalidate) instruction wrappers
data.body:
## Summary

Add Rust wrappers for AArch64 TLBI system instructions at EL1, providing APIs for TLB invalidation that are currently missing from the crate.

- **No-operand**: `vmalle1()`, `vmalle1is()` — invalidate all EL1&0 TLB entries
- **VA-based**: `vae1()`, `vale1()`, `vaae1()`, `vaale1()` + IS variants — invalidate by virtual address
- **ASID-based**: `aside1()`, `aside1is()` — invalidate by ASID

## Motivation

The crate already wraps DSB/ISB barrier instructions but has no TLBI support, forcing downstream users to write inline assembly for TLB maintenance. For example, [SimpleKernel](https://github.com/Simple-XX/SimpleKernel) currently uses raw `asm!("tlbi vmalle1", "dsb sy", "isb")` in its memory management code because no wrapper is available.

## Design

- Follows the existing pattern from `barrier.rs` and `asm.rs`: `#[inline(always)]`, `match ()` with `#[cfg(target_arch)]`, `options(nostack)`, `unimplemented!()` fallback for non-aarch64 targets.
- Functions are safe (not `unsafe fn`), consistent with existing instruction wrappers in this crate (`nop()`, `wfi()`, `dsb()`, etc.). The `unsafe` block is scoped to the `asm!()` call inside each function body.
- Each function documents the operand encoding per Arm ARM §C5.5 / §D8.10.
- Register-operand instructions (`vae1`, `vale1`, etc.) accept a raw `u64` operand — the caller is responsible for encoding ASID and VA fields correctly, keeping the wrapper thin and flexible.
- Module docs include a `# Synchronization` section noting that callers must use appropriate DSB/ISB barriers, with a complete example.

## Verification

All 12 instructions verified via cross-compilation + disassembly (`aarch64-linux-gnu-objdump`):

| Instruction | Opcode | Operand |
|---|---|---|
| `tlbi vmalle1` | `d508871f` | none (xzr) |
| `tlbi vmalle1is` | `d508831f` | none (xzr) |
| `tlbi vae1, x0` | `d5088720` | register |
| `tlbi vae1is, x0` | `d5088320` | register |
| `tlbi vale1, x0` | `d50887a0` | register |
| `tlbi vale1is, x0` | `d50883a0` | register |
| `tlbi vaae1, x0` | `d5088760` | register |
| `tlbi vaae1is, x0` | `d5088360` | register |
| `tlbi vaale1, x0` | `d50887e0` | register |
| `tlbi vaale1is, x0` | `d50883e0` | register |
| `tlbi aside1, x0` | `d5088740` | register |
| `tlbi aside1is, x0` | `d5088340` | register |

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build --target aarch64-unknown-none-softfloat` — compiles
- [x] `cargo build` (host target) — compiles
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` — passes
- [x] `cargo clippy --target aarch64-unknown-none-softfloat` — no warnings
- [x] Cross-compiled test crate + `objdump` — all 12 opcodes match ARM ARM encoding

data.title: Add DAIFSet and DAIFClr register definitions
data.body:
## Summary

- Add `DAIFSet` and `DAIFClr` write-only pseudo-register wrappers that emit the atomic `MSR DAIFSet, #<imm>` / `MSR DAIFClr, #<imm>` instructions
- These allow individual DAIF bits to be set (masked) or cleared (unmasked) without a read-modify-write sequence on the whole `DAIF` register, which could accidentally unmask unrelated exception types (Debug/SError/FIQ)
- Field definitions use `NoEffect = 0` / `Mask = 1` (or `Unmask = 1`) enum values, consistent with the existing `DAIF` register style

## Motivation

The existing `DAIF` register provides whole-register access via `MSR DAIF, <Xt>`. To mask only IRQ, a caller must read DAIF, OR in the I bit, then write it back — but between the read and write, an interrupt could change DAIF, causing a race. The `DAIFSet`/`DAIFClr` instructions are specifically designed by the Arm architecture to avoid this: they atomically set/clear selected bits in a single instruction.

Currently, downstream users (e.g., OS kernels) must resort to raw inline assembly (`asm!("msr daifset, #2")`) because the crate doesn't expose these pseudo-registers. This PR fills that gap.

## Usage

```rust
use aarch64_cpu::registers::*;

// Mask IRQ (equivalent to: asm!("msr daifset, #2"))
DAIFSet.write(DAIFSet::I::Mask);

// Unmask IRQ (equivalent to: asm!("msr daifclr, #2"))
DAIFClr.write(DAIFClr::I::Unmask);

// Mask IRQ + FIQ together
DAIFSet.write(DAIFSet::I::Mask + DAIFSet::F::Mask);
```

## Implementation notes

Since `MSR DAIFSet/DAIFClr` takes a 4-bit immediate (not a register operand), the standard `sys_coproc_write_raw!` macro cannot be used. Instead, the `Writeable::set()` implementation matches on all 16 possible immediate values (0x0–0xF) and emits the corresponding instruction with a constant immediate. The compiler optimizes this to a single instruction when the value is known at compile time.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo fmt -- --check` passes
- [x] `cargo clippy --target=aarch64-unknown-none-softfloat` passes
- [x] `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build --target=aarch64-unknown-none-softfloat` passes
- [x] `cargo check --target=aarch64-unknown-none` passes (cross-compile)
- [x] `cargo check` passes (host, non-aarch64 path with `unimplemented!()`)

data.message:
Add DAIFSet and DAIFClr register definitions

The existing DAIF register only supports whole-register read/write via
MSR DAIF, <Xt>, which can accidentally unmask unrelated exception types
(Debug/SError/FIQ) during a read-modify-write sequence.

Add DAIFSet and DAIFClr pseudo-register wrappers that emit the atomic
MSR DAIFSet/DAIFClr immediate instructions, allowing individual DAIF
bits to be set or cleared without affecting other bits.

feat(sched): implement CFS scheduler with priority inheritance

Add completely fair scheduler (CFS) with priority inheritance protocol
to prevent priority inversion in real-time tasks. Includes red-black
tree based run queue and virtual runtime accounting.

fix(mm): resolve use-after-free in slab allocator on SMP

Fix race condition where cache reaping could free a slab while another
CPU held a pointer to an object within it. Add per-CPU lock and
validate cache state before reclamation.

data.title: feat: Add virtio-blk driver with async I/O support
data.body:
## Summary

Implements a virtio-blk block device driver with full async I/O support using the kernel's async task system.

## Changes
- VirtIO split virtqueue implementation
- DMA-safe buffer management with bounce buffers for non-IOMMU platforms
- Block layer integration with pluggable scheduler (noop/deadline)
- Benchmarks: 420k IOPS on 4KB random reads vs 180k with synchronous driver

## Testing
- Tested on QEMU virt machine (RISC-V and x86_64)
- All existing fs tests pass
- Added 23 new unit tests for virtqueue management

data.title: Add support for RISC-V N extension (user-mode interrupts)
data.body:
## Feature Request

The RISC-V N extension allows user-mode software to handle interrupts directly without kernel intervention. This would be useful for implementing fast userspace interrupt handlers and could significantly reduce latency for certain device classes.

## Proposed Approach

1. Detect N extension support via `misa` CSR at boot
2. Add `ustatus`, `uie`, `uip`, `utvec`, `uepc`, `ucause`, `utval` CSR management
3. Expose via `prctl(PR_SET_USER_INTR, ...)` syscall
4. Update context switch to save/restore N extension state

Estimated scope: ~400 lines. Happy to implement if there's interest.

data.message:
fix(14_exceptions): correct ESR_EL1 decoding for data abort

Fix incorrect bit mask for ISS field extraction in data abort handler.
Previous mask `0x1FFFFFF` was one bit too wide; should be `0xFFFFFF`.
This caused spurious "unknown fault" messages on unaligned accesses.

data.title: fix: repair SMP percpu init race on AP startup
data.body:
Fixes #92.

## Root Cause

`percpu_area_init()` was being invoked by Application Processors before the BSP had finished setting up the percpu memory region. The AP trampoline code lacked a synchronization barrier.

## Fix

Add a spinlock-based barrier (`smp_percpu_ready`) that APs spin on until the BSP sets it after completing `percpu_area_init()`. The barrier uses the same MESI-coherent memory that the SMP init already depends on, so no new cache concerns.

## Tested
- QEMU with 1/2/4/8 vCPUs — no more panics
- Stress-tested with 1000 boot iterations using a shell script

data.message:
feat(ipc): add POSIX message queue implementation

Implement mq_open, mq_send, mq_receive, mq_close, mq_unlink syscalls
per POSIX.1-2017. Uses priority-sorted linked list internally.
Supports blocking and non-blocking modes, notification via SIGEV_SIGNAL.

data.body: 思路完全正确，COW fork 的核心逻辑没问题。主要的 concern 是 page fault handler 里的 CoW 路径和 swap-in 路径共用了同一把锁，在高内存压力下可能死锁。建议把两个路径的锁粒度分开。

inlineComments.body: `vma.lock()` 在这里持有时间太长了，整个 `do_cow_fault` 流程都在锁里面。可以只在修改 PTE 的时候加锁，分配物理页的操作不需要持有 VMA 锁。
如果 `alloc_page()` 在这里返回 `None`（OOM），应该触发 OOM killer 而不是直接 panic。

data.issueTitle: Performance regression in pipe read/write after #801
data.body:
我看了一下，问题出在 #801 把 pipe 的内部缓冲从栈上的 `[u8; 4096]` 改成了 `Vec<u8>`，导致每次 `pipe_read` 都会触发堆分配。

最简单的修法是用 `Box<[u8; PIPE_BUF]>` 替代 `Vec`，分配一次之后就不会再触发 allocator。或者更激进一点，用 `VecDeque` 加对象池。我倾向于前者，改动更小，性能恢复也够用。

data.issueTitle: Add support for RISC-V N extension (user-mode interrupts)
data.body: @contributor-xyz 好的，这个 feature 我们有兴趣支持。不过要注意 N 扩展在最新的 RISC-V spec 里已经被标记为 ratified 但实现很稀少，主要的参考实现只有 QEMU 和少数 FPGA 平台。建议先在 QEMU 上实现和测试，同时把代码写成 feature flag 可选，避免影响不支持该扩展的目标平台。

data.message:
feat(syscall): implement execve with proper ELF loader

Full ELF64 loader supporting LOAD segments, dynamic linking via ld.so,
and interpreter execution. Handles AT_* auxiliary vector population.
Now possible to run musl-libc compiled binaries.

tmp (#201)

* refactor: migrate Singleton<T> to etl::singleton<T> named aliases

Replace custom Singleton<T> with etl::singleton<T> using per-type
named aliases (e.g. BasicInfoSingleton, TaskManagerSingleton) defined
centrally in kernel.h. Delete singleton.hpp. Update all call sites
from Singleton<T>::GetInstance() to TypeSingleton::instance() and
assignment-construction to TypeSingleton::create(). Update all
documentation references.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): move arch-specific singletons out of kernel.h and make interrupt controllers Interrupt members

Move arch-specific singleton type aliases from shared kernel.h into
per-arch directories, and convert interrupt controller singletons
(PlicSingleton, ApicSingleton) into private members of each arch's
Interrupt class, following the existing aarch64 pattern where Gic is
already an Interrupt member.

- Move Pl011Singleton to src/arch/aarch64/include/pl011_singleton.h
- Move SerialSingleton to file-local scope in x86_64/early_console.cpp
- Move Ns16550aSingleton to file-local scope in riscv64/interrupt_main.cpp
- Add Plic plic_ member to riscv64 Interrupt with InitPlic() deferred init
- Add Apic apic_ member to x86_64 Interrupt with InitApic() deferred init
- Move APIC creation from ArchInit() to InterruptInit() (boot order fix)
- Remove arch-specific #includes and #ifdefs from kernel.h

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): remove static from Interrupt handler arrays

Since Interrupt is used as etl::singleton (only one instance), static
class members are semantically equivalent to non-static members. Remove
static to eliminate the need for out-of-class definitions in .cpp files.

- aarch64: interrupt_handlers -> interrupt_handlers_ (non-static member)
- riscv64: interrupt_handlers_, exception_handlers_ (drop static + defs)
- x86_64: interrupt_handlers_, idts_ (drop static + defs, keep alignas)

The alignas(4096) on x86_64 members propagates correctly through
etl::singleton via uninitialized_buffer_of<T> which uses
alignas(etl::alignment_of<T>::value) on its storage.

* feat(device): add DmaRegion type and IoBuffer::ToDmaRegion()

Introduce DmaRegion{virt, phys, size} value type, VirtToPhysFunc callback, and IdentityVirtToPhys default in io_buffer.hpp. Add ToDmaRegion() to IoBuffer for converting buffers to DMA-ready regions. Includes 8 GoogleTest unit tests and implementation plan.

* refactor(device): replace void* DMA with DmaRegion + VirtToPhys abstraction

Refactor SplitVirtqueue, VirtioBlk, and VirtioDriver to use DmaRegion instead of raw void*/uintptr_t pairs. VirtioBlk::Create() now accepts separate vq_dma and slot_dma regions with a VirtToPhysFunc callback, eliminating hardcoded identity-mapping assumptions. RequestSlot memory is allocated from a dedicated DMA buffer for device-accessible physical addresses.

data.title: tmp

data.message:
sync with fork (#200)

* fix: update unique_ptr

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor: migrate Singleton<T> to etl::singleton<T> named aliases

Replace custom Singleton<T> with etl::singleton<T> using per-type
named aliases (e.g. BasicInfoSingleton, TaskManagerSingleton) defined
centrally in kernel.h. Delete singleton.hpp. Update all call sites
from Singleton<T>::GetInstance() to TypeSingleton::instance() and
assignment-construction to TypeSingleton::create(). Update all
documentation references.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): move arch-specific singletons out of kernel.h and make interrupt controllers Interrupt members

Move arch-specific singleton type aliases from shared kernel.h into
per-arch directories, and convert interrupt controller singletons
(PlicSingleton, ApicSingleton) into private members of each arch's
Interrupt class, following the existing aarch64 pattern where Gic is
already an Interrupt member.

- Move Pl011Singleton to src/arch/aarch64/include/pl011_singleton.h
- Move SerialSingleton to file-local scope in x86_64/early_console.cpp
- Move Ns16550aSingleton to file-local scope in riscv64/interrupt_main.cpp
- Add Plic plic_ member to riscv64 Interrupt with InitPlic() deferred init
- Add Apic apic_ member to x86_64 Interrupt with InitApic() deferred init
- Move APIC creation from ArchInit() to InterruptInit() (boot order fix)
- Remove arch-specific #includes and #ifdefs from kernel.h

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): remove static from Interrupt handler arrays

Since Interrupt is used as etl::singleton (only one instance), static
class members are semantically equivalent to non-static members. Remove
static to eliminate the need for out-of-class definitions in .cpp files.

- aarch64: interrupt_handlers -> interrupt_handlers_ (non-static member)
- riscv64: interrupt_handlers_, exception_handlers_ (drop static + defs)
- x86_64: interrupt_handlers_, idts_ (drop static + defs, keep alignas)

The alignas(4096) on x86_64 members propagates correctly through
etl::singleton via uninitialized_buffer_of<T> which uses
alignas(etl::alignment_of<T>::value) on its storage.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* 3rd: add cpu_io

* refactor(virtio): modernize VirtioBlk with std::array

Replace raw C arrays slots_[kMaxInflight] with std::array<RequestSlot, kMaxInflight>
and local IoVec[] with std::array<IoVec, kMaxSgElements> in DoEnqueue().
Replace static kNames[] with constexpr std::array<const char*, 4> in GetName().
Also zero-initialize slot_bitmap_ explicitly with {}.

Note: std::bitset was evaluated but omitted — std::bitset<N>::to_ulong()
references exception machinery (__throw_out_of_range_fmt) in GCC 14's
libstdc++, which is incompatible with this freestanding kernel build.

data.title: sync with fork

data.message:
fix(test): use sys_yield() instead of sys_sleep() in balance system test (#199)

* refactor(arch): move arch-specific singletons out of kernel.h and make interrupt controllers Interrupt members

Move arch-specific singleton type aliases from shared kernel.h into
per-arch directories, and convert interrupt controller singletons
(PlicSingleton, ApicSingleton) into private members of each arch's
Interrupt class, following the existing aarch64 pattern where Gic is
already an Interrupt member.

- Move Pl011Singleton to src/arch/aarch64/include/pl011_singleton.h
- Move SerialSingleton to file-local scope in x86_64/early_console.cpp
- Move Ns16550aSingleton to file-local scope in riscv64/interrupt_main.cpp
- Add Plic plic_ member to riscv64 Interrupt with InitPlic() deferred init
- Add Apic apic_ member to x86_64 Interrupt with InitApic() deferred init
- Move APIC creation from ArchInit() to InterruptInit() (boot order fix)
- Remove arch-specific #includes and #ifdefs from kernel.h

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): remove static from Interrupt handler arrays

Since Interrupt is used as etl::singleton (only one instance), static
class members are semantically equivalent to non-static members. Remove
static to eliminate the need for out-of-class definitions in .cpp files.

- aarch64: interrupt_handlers -> interrupt_handlers_ (non-static member)
- riscv64: interrupt_handlers_, exception_handlers_ (drop static + defs)
- x86_64: interrupt_handlers_, idts_ (drop static + defs, keep alignas)

The alignas(4096) on x86_64 members propagates correctly through
etl::singleton via uninitialized_buffer_of<T> which uses
alignas(etl::alignment_of<T>::value) on its storage.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(device): delete placeholder and redundant header files

Inline PlatformTraits struct into platform_config.hpp and delete
the now-redundant platform_traits.hpp. Also delete zero-caller
stubs: acpi_bus.hpp, pci_bus.hpp, virtio_messages.hpp.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(device): simplify device module — remove Driver concept and variant types

Replace template Register<D>() + Driver concept with plain DriverEntry
function-pointer struct. Flatten DeviceNode to pure data (remove variant
BusId, DeviceResource, DMA buffer, SpinLock). Move DMA allocation into

* refactor(virtio): modernize VirtioBlk with std::array

Replace raw C arrays slots_[kMaxInflight] with std::array<RequestSlot, kMaxInflight>
and local IoVec[] with std::array<IoVec, kMaxSgElements> in DoEnqueue().
Replace static kNames[] with constexpr std::array<const char*, 4> in GetName().
Also zero-initialize slot_bitmap_ explicitly with {}.

Note: std::bitset was evaluated but omitted — std::bitset<N>::to_ulong()
references exception machinery (__throw_out_of_range_fmt) in GCC 14's
libstdc++, which is incompatible with this freestanding kernel build.

* fix(ci): detect riscv64 system test failures by checking output

The riscv64 system test step only checked the cmake exit code, which
is always 0 even when individual tests fail inside QEMU. Align with
the aarch64 approach: capture output to file, grep for "Failed: 0"
to determine pass/fail.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* feat: balance

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* fix(test): use batch yield+sleep to avoid recursive sched_lock panic

Workers in tight sys_yield() loops create extreme scheduling pressure,
triggering a recursive spinlock panic on sched_lock. The window between
Schedule()'s UnLock (interrupts restored) and switch_to allows a timer
interrupt to re-enter the scheduler path.

Fix: batch 10 yields (visible to Balance()) with 1ms sleeps (reduces
lock contention). Total lifetime still covers multiple Balance() cycles.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* fix(ci): detect hung tests by grepping for definitive success marker

Previously CI grepped for 'Failed: 0' which matches even when tests
time out (e.g. 'Failed: 0 | Timeout: 1'), silently passing a hung test.

Now the kernel test runner prints 'RESULT: ALL TESTS PASSED' only when
every test passes with no failures and no timeouts. CI greps for this
marker instead, correctly catching all failure modes:
- Test assertion failures (no marker printed)
- Kernel PANIC/deadlock (no output at all, timeout 300 kills QEMU)
- Individual test hangs (runner marks as Timeout, marker not printed)

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>
Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

data.title: fix(test): use sys_yield() instead of sys_sleep() in balance system test
data.body:
## Summary

- **修复 balance system test 失败问题**：将 worker 的 `sys_sleep()` 替换为 `sys_yield()`，使任务对负载均衡器可见

## 问题原因

`Balance()` 通过 `GetQueueSize()` 检查各核心的调度器就绪队列来判断负载。但 `sys_sleep()` 会将任务从就绪队列移到独立的 `sleeping_tasks` 优先队列中，导致：

1. Worker 调用 `sys_sleep(10)` → 移出 `ready_queue_`，进入 `sleeping_tasks`
2. `Balance()` 每 64 tick 运行，检查 `GetQueueSize()` → 返回 0（sleeping 任务不可见）
3. 所有核心的 ready queue 看起来都是空的 → 不触发任何迁移
4. 所有 worker 始终在同一核心执行 → `cores_used < 2` → 测试失败

## 修复方案

- `imbalance_worker`: `sys_sleep(10)` → `sys_yield()`，迭代次数 20 → 2000
- `affinity_pinned_worker`: `sys_sleep(10)` → `sys_yield()`，迭代次数 10 → 1000
- `sys_yield()` 调用 `Schedule()` 后任务留在就绪队列中，对 `Balance()` 可见
- 增大迭代次数确保 worker 存活时间覆盖多个 Balance 周期（每 64ms 一次）

## 测试

- `make SimpleKernel` 编译通过（riscv64）

fix(ci): add safe.directory config for container submodule checkout (#198)

* fix: update unique_ptr

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor: migrate Singleton<T> to etl::singleton<T> named aliases

Replace custom Singleton<T> with etl::singleton<T> using per-type
named aliases (e.g. BasicInfoSingleton, TaskManagerSingleton) defined
centrally in kernel.h. Delete singleton.hpp. Update all call sites
from Singleton<T>::GetInstance() to TypeSingleton::instance() and
assignment-construction to TypeSingleton::create(). Update all
documentation references.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): move arch-specific singletons out of kernel.h and make interrupt controllers Interrupt members

Move arch-specific singleton type aliases from shared kernel.h into
per-arch directories, and convert interrupt controller singletons
(PlicSingleton, ApicSingleton) into private members of each arch's
Interrupt class, following the existing aarch64 pattern where Gic is
already an Interrupt member.

- Move Pl011Singleton to src/arch/aarch64/include/pl011_singleton.h
- Move SerialSingleton to file-local scope in x86_64/early_console.cpp
- Move Ns16550aSingleton to file-local scope in riscv64/interrupt_main.cpp
- Add Plic plic_ member to riscv64 Interrupt with InitPlic() deferred init
- Add Apic apic_ member to x86_64 Interrupt with InitApic() deferred init
- Move APIC creation from ArchInit() to InterruptInit() (boot order fix)
- Remove arch-specific #includes and #ifdefs from kernel.h

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): remove static from Interrupt handler arrays

Since Interrupt is used as etl::singleton (only one instance), static
class members are semantically equivalent to non-static members. Remove
static to eliminate the need for out-of-class definitions in .cpp files.

- aarch64: interrupt_handlers -> interrupt_handlers_ (non-static member)
- riscv64: interrupt_handlers_, exception_handlers_ (drop static + defs)
- x86_64: interrupt_handlers_, idts_ (drop static + defs, keep alignas)

The alignas(4096) on x86_64 members propagates correctly through
etl::singleton via uninitialized_buffer_of<T> which uses
alignas(etl::alignment_of<T>::value) on its storage.

* refactor(virtio): modernize VirtioBlk with std::array

Replace raw C arrays slots_[kMaxInflight] with std::array<RequestSlot, kMaxInflight>
and local IoVec[] with std::array<IoVec, kMaxSgElements> in DoEnqueue().
Replace static kNames[] with constexpr std::array<const char*, 4> in GetName().
Also zero-initialize slot_bitmap_ explicitly with {}.

Note: std::bitset was evaluated but omitted — std::bitset<N>::to_ulong()
references exception machinery (__throw_out_of_range_fmt) in GCC 14's
libstdc++, which is incompatible with this freestanding kernel build.

* fix(ci): add safe.directory config for container submodule checkout

When running inside a container job, git submodule update fails with
exit code 128 due to directory ownership mismatch (container runs as
root, workspace owned by runner user). Adding safe.directory wildcard
before submodule init resolves this.

* fix(ci): detect riscv64 system test failures by checking output

The riscv64 system test step only checked the cmake exit code, which
is always 0 even when individual tests fail inside QEMU. Align with
the aarch64 approach: capture output to file, grep for "Failed: 0"
to determine pass/fail.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

data.title: fix(ci): add safe.directory config for container submodule checkout
data.body:
## Summary

修复 CI 流水线两个问题：

### 1. `Shallow submodule init` exit code 128
使用 `container:` 运行 job 时，容器以 root 用户运行，但 `actions/checkout` 创建的工作目录归属于 runner 用户。`git submodule update` 在子目录中触发 git 的 `safe.directory` 检查，导致 exit code 128。

**Fix**: 在两个 build job 的 submodule init 步骤前添加 `git config --global --add safe.directory '*'`

### 2. riscv64 system test 无法检测到测试失败
riscv64 的 system test 步骤只检查 cmake 退出码（始终为 0），即使 QEMU 内的测试失败也会报告 passed。

**Fix**: 与 aarch64 对齐，捕获输出到文件并 grep `"Failed: 0"` 来判断通过/失败。

## Failed runs
- https://github.com/Simple-XX/SimpleKernel/actions/runs/23351172436 (safe.directory)
- riscv64 `balance_test` FAIL 但 CI 报告 passed (test detection)

* refactor: migrate Singleton<T> to etl::singleton<T> named aliases

Replace custom Singleton<T> with etl::singleton<T> using per-type
named aliases (e.g. BasicInfoSingleton, TaskManagerSingleton) defined
centrally in kernel.h. Delete singleton.hpp. Update all call sites
from Singleton<T>::GetInstance() to TypeSingleton::instance() and
assignment-construction to TypeSingleton::create(). Update all
documentation references.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): move arch-specific singletons out of kernel.h and make interrupt controllers Interrupt members

Move arch-specific singleton type aliases from shared kernel.h into
per-arch directories, and convert interrupt controller singletons
(PlicSingleton, ApicSingleton) into private members of each arch's
Interrupt class, following the existing aarch64 pattern where Gic is
already an Interrupt member.

- Move Pl011Singleton to src/arch/aarch64/include/pl011_singleton.h
- Move SerialSingleton to file-local scope in x86_64/early_console.cpp
- Move Ns16550aSingleton to file-local scope in riscv64/interrupt_main.cpp
- Add Plic plic_ member to riscv64 Interrupt with InitPlic() deferred init
- Add Apic apic_ member to x86_64 Interrupt with InitApic() deferred init
- Move APIC creation from ArchInit() to InterruptInit() (boot order fix)
- Remove arch-specific #includes and #ifdefs from kernel.h

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): remove static from Interrupt handler arrays

Since Interrupt is used as etl::singleton (only one instance), static
class members are semantically equivalent to non-static members. Remove
static to eliminate the need for out-of-class definitions in .cpp files.

- aarch64: interrupt_handlers -> interrupt_handlers_ (non-static member)
- riscv64: interrupt_handlers_, exception_handlers_ (drop static + defs)
- x86_64: interrupt_handlers_, idts_ (drop static + defs, keep alignas)

The alignas(4096) on x86_64 members propagates correctly through
etl::singleton via uninitialized_buffer_of<T> which uses
alignas(etl::alignment_of<T>::value) on its storage.

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(virtio): modernize VirtioBlk with std::array

Replace raw C arrays slots_[kMaxInflight] with std::array<RequestSlot, kMaxInflight>
and local IoVec[] with std::array<IoVec, kMaxSgElements> in DoEnqueue().
Replace static kNames[] with constexpr std::array<const char*, 4> in GetName().
Also zero-initialize slot_bitmap_ explicitly with {}.

Note: std::bitset was evaluated but omitted — std::bitset<N>::to_ulong()
references exception machinery (__throw_out_of_range_fmt) in GCC 14's
libstdc++, which is incompatible with this freestanding kernel build.

data.title: perf(ci): parallelize jobs, prebuilt Docker image, caching and shallow clone
data.body:
## Summary

CI 流程优化，通过并行化、预构建镜像、缓存和浅克隆大幅减少 CI 耗时。

## Changes

### `workflow.yml` — 主构建 workflow 重写

- **并行 jobs**: 将单个串行 job 拆分为 `build-riscv64` + `build-aarch64` 并行执行，`publish` 等待两者完成
- **预构建容器**: 用 `container: ghcr.io/simple-xx/simplekernel-dev:latest` 替代每个 step 调用 `devcontainers/ci@v0.3`，消除重复容器启动开销
- **浅克隆**: `fetch-depth: 1` + `git submodule update --depth 1`，减少 git clone 时间
- **CMake 缓存**: `actions/cache@v4` 缓存 `build_{arch}/` 目录，支持增量编译
- **动态 system test 次数**: PR 跑 3 次，push/release 跑 10 次
- **Concurrency**: 同 branch/PR 的旧 run 自动取消
- **依赖升级**: `codecov-action` v3→v4, `actions-gh-pages` v3→v4
- **Publish 优化**: 通过 artifact 传递 docs，publish job 不需要 checkout 整个仓库

### `dev-image.yml` — 新增 Docker 镜像构建 workflow

- 仅在 `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` 或自身变更时触发，支持 `workflow_dispatch` 手动触发
- 构建镜像推送到 `ghcr.io/simple-xx/simplekernel-dev:latest` 和 `:<sha>` 双 tag
- 使用 Docker Buildx + GitHub Actions Cache 加速镜像构建

## Expected Impact

| 优化项 | 预估收益 |
|-------|---------|
| 并行 jobs | 总时间约减半 |
| 预构建 Docker 镜像 | 每次省 3-5 min |
| 浅克隆 + shallow submodules | 省 1-3 min |
| CMake 缓存 | 增量编译省 2-5 min |
| PR 减少 system test 次数 | PR 阶段省 50-70% system test 时间 |
| Concurrency cancel | 避免无用 run 浪费资源 |

## Note

首次合并后需手动触发一次 `dev-image` workflow（Actions → dev-image → Run workflow）来构建初始镜像，之后 `build` workflow 才能正常拉取容器。

build(ci): migrate CI from Docker image to devcontainers/ci (#196)

Replace addnab/docker-run-action with devcontainers/ci@v0.3 to use
the project's .devcontainer/Dockerfile (GCC 14) instead of the outdated

data.title: build(ci): migrate CI from Docker image to devcontainers/ci
data.body:
## Summary

- 将 CI 从 `addnab/docker-run-action` + `ptrnull233/simple_kernel:latest`（GCC 13）迁移到 `devcontainers/ci@v0.3`
- CI 现在使用项目的 `.devcontainer/Dockerfile`（GCC 14），与本地开发环境一致

## Motivation

PR #195 合入后 CI 构建失败，因为代码使用了 C++23 deducing this（`this auto self`）语法，需要 GCC 14+，但旧 Docker 镜像仅包含 GCC 13。

## Changes

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| `addnab/docker-run-action@v3` | `devcontainers/ci@v0.3` |
| `ptrnull233/simple_kernel:latest` (GCC 13) | `.devcontainer/Dockerfile` (GCC 14) |
| 环境变量传递 `CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE` | 使用 preset 默认配置 |

data.title: docs(devcontainer): migrate from Docker to Dev Container workflow
data.body:
## Summary

- **新增** `.devcontainer/Dockerfile`：基于 Ubuntu，包含 GCC 14 全架构交叉编译工具链（x86_64/riscv64/aarch64/arm）、QEMU、CMake、GDB 等
- **更新** `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json`：从预构建镜像切换为本地 Dockerfile 构建，添加 VS Code C++23/C23 配置和精简的扩展列表
- **重写** `docs/docker.md`：从 Docker 手动操作指南改为 Dev Container 文档，覆盖 VS Code / GitHub Codespaces / CLI 三种使用方式
- **更新** `README.md`、`README_ENG.md`、`docs/0_工具链.md`：所有 Docker 相关描述统一迁移为 Dev Container

## Motivation

旧的 Docker 工作流需要手动 `docker pull/run`、配置 SSH、端口映射等步骤。Dev Container 提供声明式配置，VS Code 一键打开、Codespaces 零配置，大幅降低环境搭建门槛。

per-arch directories, and convert interrupt controller singletons

- Move SerialSingleton to file-local scope in x86_64/early_console.cpp
- Move Ns16550aSingleton to file-local scope in riscv64/interrupt_main.cpp
- Add Plic plic_ member to riscv64 Interrupt with InitPlic() deferred init
- Add Apic apic_ member to x86_64 Interrupt with InitApic() deferred init
- Move APIC creation from ArchInit() to InterruptInit() (boot order fix)
- Remove arch-specific #includes and #ifdefs from kernel.h

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): remove static from Interrupt handler arrays

Since Interrupt is used as etl::singleton (only one instance), static
class members are semantically equivalent to non-static members. Remove
static to eliminate the need for out-of-class definitions in .cpp files.

- aarch64: interrupt_handlers -> interrupt_handlers_ (non-static member)
- riscv64: interrupt_handlers_, exception_handlers_ (drop static + defs)
- x86_64: interrupt_handlers_, idts_ (drop static + defs, keep alignas)

The alignas(4096) on x86_64 members propagates correctly through
etl::singleton via uninitialized_buffer_of<T> which uses
alignas(etl::alignment_of<T>::value) on its storage.

* refactor(virtio): modernize VirtioBlk with std::array

Replace raw C arrays slots_[kMaxInflight] with std::array<RequestSlot, kMaxInflight>
and local IoVec[] with std::array<IoVec, kMaxSgElements> in DoEnqueue().
Replace static kNames[] with constexpr std::array<const char*, 4> in GetName().
Also zero-initialize slot_bitmap_ explicitly with {}.

Note: std::bitset was evaluated but omitted — std::bitset<N>::to_ulong()
references exception machinery (__throw_out_of_range_fmt) in GCC 14's
libstdc++, which is incompatible with this freestanding kernel build.

data.title: feat: Copilot review fixes, AGENTS.md docs, coding standards, and test improvements
data.body:
## Summary

- **fix(multi)**: Address Copilot review findings across IO APIC, VFS, device, and DMA subsystems
- **docs**: Update AGENTS.md with comprehensive module documentation
- **style**: Apply coding standards — trailing return types, `pragma once`, consistent formatting across all `.cpp` and headers
- **test**: Fix system test issues and improve test coverage

## Details

### Bug Fixes
- Fix warnings across the codebase
- Address review findings in IO APIC, VFS, device framework, and DMA region handling

### Style & Standards
- Migrate headers to `pragma once`
- Apply trailing return types in `.cpp` files and `arch.h`
- Update headers to follow `docs/coding_standards.md` conventions

### Documentation
- Update `AGENTS.md` with current module structure, code map, and conventions

### Tests
- Fix system test failures
- Improve test stability

feat: ETL migration, MPMC logging, device framework overhaul, VFS/FatFS, and coding standards (#193)

* refactor(main): use make_unique for test task creation

Replace raw new TaskControlBlock with sk_std::make_unique and .release()
before passing to AddTask. Ensures exception-safe construction while
maintaining AddTask's raw pointer interface.

Add comprehensive ETL migration plan (Tasks 0-10) for replacing sk_std:: containers with ETL-backed kstd:: wrappers. Remove superseded unique_ptr design/implementation docs.

* feat(libcxx): add kstd container wrappers for ETL

Add kstd namespace wrappers around ETL containers:
- kstd_pool.hpp: Pool<T,N> and PoolRef type aliases
- kstd_vector: vector, static_vector, ivector
- kstd_list: list, static_list, list_node_t
- kstd_unique_ptr: custom unique_ptr implementation
- kstd_priority_queue: reference-based priority queue
- kstd_unordered_map: pool-backed unordered_map with std::hash

Ultraworked with [Sisyphus](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode)

Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>

* refactor(libcxx): convert sk_std headers to kstd shims

Replace sk_std:: implementations with thin forwarding shims to kstd::
namespace. sk_vector, sk_list, sk_unique_ptr, sk_priority_queue, and
sk_unordered_map now delegate to ETL-backed kstd wrappers.

Ultraworked with [Sisyphus](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode)

Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>

* refactor(task): migrate task subsystem to kstd containers

Replace sk_std:: containers with kstd:: static containers in schedulers
and task manager. Use static_vector, static_list, and static_unordered_map
with compile-time sizes. Mark scheduler copy/move as deleted since ETL
containers with internal storage are not copyable.

Ultraworked with [Sisyphus](https://github.com/code-yeongyu/oh-my-opencode)

Co-authored-by: Sisyphus <clio-agent@sisyphuslabs.ai>

* refactor: migrate Singleton<T> to etl::singleton<T> named aliases

Replace custom Singleton<T> with etl::singleton<T> using per-type
named aliases (e.g. BasicInfoSingleton, TaskManagerSingleton) defined
centrally in kernel.h. Delete singleton.hpp. Update all call sites
from Singleton<T>::GetInstance() to TypeSingleton::instance() and
assignment-construction to TypeSingleton::create(). Update all
documentation references.

* refactor(arch): move arch-specific singletons out of kernel.h and make interrupt controllers Interrupt members

Move arch-specific singleton type aliases from shared kernel.h into
per-arch directories, and convert interrupt controller singletons
(PlicSingleton, ApicSingleton) into private members of each arch's
Interrupt class, following the existing aarch64 pattern where Gic is
already an Interrupt member.

- Move Pl011Singleton to src/arch/aarch64/include/pl011_singleton.h
- Move SerialSingleton to file-local scope in x86_64/early_console.cpp
- Move Ns16550aSingleton to file-local scope in riscv64/interrupt_main.cpp
- Add Plic plic_ member to riscv64 Interrupt with InitPlic() deferred init
- Add Apic apic_ member to x86_64 Interrupt with InitApic() deferred init
- Move APIC creation from ArchInit() to InterruptInit() (boot order fix)
- Remove arch-specific #includes and #ifdefs from kernel.h

Signed-off-by: Niu Zhihong <zhihong@nzhnb.com>

* refactor(arch): remove static from Interrupt handler arrays

Since Interrupt is used as etl::singleton (only one instance), static
class members are semantically equivalent to non-static members. Remove
static to eliminate the need for out-of-class definitions in .cpp files.

- aarch64: interrupt_handlers -> interrupt_handlers_ (non-static member)
- riscv64: interrupt_handlers_, exception_handlers_ (drop static + defs)
- x86_64: interrupt_handlers_, idts_ (drop static + defs, keep alignas)

The alignas(4096) on x86_64 members propagates correctly through
etl::singleton via uninitialized_buffer_of<T> which uses
alignas(etl::alignment_of<T>::value) on its storage.

* refactor(virtio): modernize VirtioBlk with std::array

Replace raw C arrays slots_[kMaxInflight] with std::array<RequestSlot, kMaxInflight>
and local IoVec[] with std::array<IoVec, kMaxSgElements> in DoEnqueue().
Replace static kNames[] with constexpr std::array<const char*, 4> in GetName().
Also zero-initialize slot_bitmap_ explicitly with {}.

Note: std::bitset was evaluated but omitted — std::bitset<N>::to_ulong()
references exception machinery (__throw_out_of_range_fmt) in GCC 14's
libstdc++, which is incompatible with this freestanding kernel build.

data.title: feat: ETL migration, MPMC logging, device framework overhaul, VFS/FatFS, and coding standards
data.body:
## Summary

Major refactoring and feature additions over the past month, focusing on replacing custom kernel infrastructure with the Embedded Template Library (ETL), rewriting the logging subsystem, overhauling the device framework, and adding a VFS/FatFS filesystem layer.

**310 files changed, +19,521 / -12,892**

---

## Key Changes

### 1. ETL (Embedded Template Library) Integration

Full migration from custom `sk_std` containers and utilities to ETL, a production-grade embedded C++ library:

- **Containers**: Replaced `sk_std::static_list`, `sk_std::vector`, `sk_std::priority_queue` with `etl::list`, `etl::vector`, `etl::priority_queue`, `etl::flat_map`
- **I/O Ports**: Migrated `io::In/Out` to `etl::io_port_ro/rw` across APIC, GIC, and VirtIO
- **Singletons**: `Singleton<T>` → `etl::singleton<T>` with named aliases in `kernel.h`
- **Delegates**: `InterruptFunc` pointer → `etl::delegate` for type-safe interrupt callbacks
- **Task FSM**: `TaskStatus` enum → `etl::fsm` state machine with well-defined transitions
- **Flags**: `CloneFlags` enum → `etl::flags` for type-safe bitmask operations
- **Observers**: Added `etl::observer`-based tick and panic interfaces
- **Messages**: Added `etl::message_router` ID registry for lifecycle and VirtIO events
- **Smart Pointers**: Added `sk_std::unique_ptr` (with array specialization) and `sk_std::shared_ptr` (with atomic reference counting)
- Removed unused `sk_std` implementations and their tests

### 2. Kernel Logging Rewrite (MPMC Lock-Free)

Complete rewrite of `kernel_log.hpp`:

- **MPMC queue**: Lock-free multi-producer multi-consumer log queue, safe for SMP use
- **ETL format syntax**: Type-safe `{}` placeholders replacing printf-style `%d`/`%s`
- **Per-CPU raw interfaces**: `klog::raw_err` / `LogLineRaw` / `LogStreamRaw` for use in contexts where locking is unsafe (e.g., `DumpStack`)
- **Core ID in logs**: Log output now includes the originating CPU core ID
- **Removed nanoprintf**: Replaced entirely with ETL's print/format facilities; added `etl_putchar` shims for all three architectures

### 3. Device Framework Overhaul

Eliminated the `device_framework` submodule and inlined all driver code:

- **Inlined from device_framework**: NS16550A, PL011, VirtIO (transport, virt_queue, device, defs), ACPI, MMIO accessor, storage, UART device, traits
- **Unified VirtioDriver**: Single `VirtioDriver` class with runtime device-type dispatch, replacing the old per-device-type classes
- **DmaRegion abstraction**: Replaced `void*` DMA buffers with typed `DmaRegion` + `VirtToPhys` translation
- **Driver restructuring**: Reorganized `src/device/` into per-driver and per-type subdirectories
- **ETL adoption in drivers**: `etl::optional`, `etl::delegate`, `etl::vector`, `etl::flat_map` in driver registry and driver implementations
- **Removed dead code**: Deleted CRTP ops/ layer, unused device wrappers, `DriverDescriptor`, `DeviceStorage`

### 4. Filesystem Layer (VFS + RamFS + FatFS)

New filesystem infrastructure:

- **VFS**: Virtual filesystem layer with `SpinLock`-guarded operations, supporting mount/open/read/write/close
- **RamFS**: In-memory filesystem using static bump pools (no heap allocation)
- **FatFS**: FatFS VFS adapter with VirtIO-blk integration via `block_device_provider`
- **FileDescriptorTable**: Per-process file descriptor management
- **System tests**: RamFS and FatFS system tests via VFS API

### 5. Memory: IoBuffer Module

- `IoBuffer`: RAII-managed aligned I/O buffers for DMA and device communication
- `dma_buffer` field in `DeviceNode` for VirtIO block driver integration
- `IoBuffer::ToDmaRegion()` for zero-copy DMA submission

### 6. SMP & Boot

- Working on SMP support: log with core ID, `boot_task` initialization, `TimerInit` moved to `main`
- Multi-string FDT compatible matching for device discovery

### 7. Style & Code Quality

- Trailing return types in `.cpp` and `arch.h`
- `#pragma once` across all headers
- `[[nodiscard]]`, `[[maybe_unused]]`, `[[gnu::xxx]]` replacing `__attribute__((xxx))`
- Coding standards enforcement across `src/arch/`, `src/filesystem/`, `src/memory/`
- Replaced `sk_assert` with standard `assert`
- Removed cppcheck (replaced by clang-tidy in pre-commit)

---

## Subsystem Diff Summary

| Subsystem | Files | Insertions | Deletions |
|-----------|-------|------------|-----------|
| `src/arch/` | 49 | +4,205 | -767 |
| `src/device/` | 35 | +4,736 | — |
| `src/filesystem/` | 32 | +4,122 | — |
| `src/task/` | 24 | +1,396 | -659 |
| `src/include/` | 20 | +1,262 | -1,037 |
| `tests/` | 62 | +4,172 | -4,204 |
| `src/memory/` | 4 | +610 | — |
| `src/libc/` + `src/libcxx/` | 29 | +225 | -2,258 |
| Build system | 9 | +153 | -182 |

## Test Plan

- [ ] `cmake --preset build_riscv64 && cd build_riscv64 && make SimpleKernel && make run`
- [ ] `cmake --preset build_aarch64 && cd build_aarch64 && make SimpleKernel && make run`
- [ ] `cmake --preset build_x86_64 && cd build_x86_64 && make SimpleKernel && make run`
- [ ] `make unit-test` on host architecture
- [ ] `pre-commit run --all-files` passes

data.title: fix: handle SIGCHLD correctly when parent is in waitpid
data.body:
## Problem

When a child process exits while the parent is blocked in `waitpid(-1, ...)`, the SIGCHLD delivery was racing with the wait queue wakeup, occasionally causing the parent to remain blocked forever.

## Fix

Ensure the wakeup happens under the `task_lock` in `do_exit()`, before the signal is dispatched. This serializes the two wakeup paths.

## Note

Closing this PR — the fix was superseded by the larger process management refactor in #212 which handles this correctly as a side effect. Changes are included there.

data.title: Stack overflow detection / guard pages not working on AArch64
data.body:
## Bug Report

Stack guard pages are configured on x86_64 but the AArch64 port doesn't set up `sp_el0` guard page mappings. When userspace overflows its stack, instead of receiving SIGSEGV it hits a data abort that isn't handled gracefully and the kernel panics.

## Steps to Reproduce

```c
void recurse(void) { char buf[4096]; recurse(); }
int main() { recurse(); }
```

Compile for AArch64 target, run in simulator → kernel panic.

## Expected

Process receives SIGSEGV and terminates cleanly.

## Environment

- Target: AArch64 QEMU virt
- Pinux commit: d4e3b991

data.message:
feat(mm): implement huge page (2MB) support for kernel mappings

Add huge page allocation and mapping in the buddy allocator. Kernel
text and rodata sections now mapped with 2MB pages on both x86_64 and
rv64, reducing TLB pressure significantly on large workloads.

data.issueTitle: Stack overflow detection / guard pages not working on AArch64
data.body:
确认可以复现。AArch64 的问题在于 `do_page_fault` 里没有对 `ESR_EL1.EC == 0x25`（data abort from lower EL）做 guard page 的特判。x86_64 有这个逻辑，但移植时漏掉了。

我在 PR #115 里顺带修了这个，可以关闭这个 issue。

Extract architecture-specific early boot (UART init, page table setup,

data.message:
fix(net): fix off-by-one in TCP sequence number wraparound

TCP sequence numbers wrap at 2^32. Comparison using plain subtraction
was incorrect when the gap exceeded 2^31. Use RFC 793 modular arithmetic
for all SEQ comparisons.

docs(15_virtual_mem_part3): clarify ASID management and TLB flush semantics

Expand explanation of why ASID rollovers require a full TLB flush and
add a diagram showing the relationship between ASID, TTBR0_EL1, and
the TLB context tags. Fixes several unclear sentences in the prose.

data.title: Add ETL_FORMAT_NO_FLOATING_POINT control macro for etl::format
data.body:
This pull request adds conditional compilation support to exclude floating-point formatting functionality from the `etl::format` module when the `ETL_FORMAT_NO_FLOATING_POINT` macro is defined. This allows the codebase to be built without floating-point dependencies, which is useful for embedded or resource-constrained environments. The changes are applied consistently across both the implementation (`include/etl/format.h`) and tests (`test/test_format.cpp`).

**Conditional floating-point support:**

* Wrapped all floating-point type support and formatting logic in `etl::format` (e.g., support for `float`, `double`, `long double`, and their formatters) with `#if !defined(ETL_FORMAT_NO_FLOATING_POINT)` guards to allow exclusion when the macro is defined.

**Test adjustments:**

* Wrapped all floating-point formatting tests in `test/test_format.cpp` with the same macro guard to ensure tests are only run when floating-point support is enabled.

See https://github.com/ETLCPP/etl/discussions/1326

data.title: Feature/floating point control macro

data.content: 二月最后一周你给 rCore 审了 6 个 PR，其中 5 个你要求修改，1 个直接 LGTM。这个通过率在代码审查界已经算是出了名的严格，建议你在 GitHub profile 上挂一个"此处审查，九死一生"的警示牌。另外你在凌晨两点提交的那个 ELF loader 修复，commit message 里有三个错别字，可见深夜 coding 对人类的伤害是真实存在的。
